slide_1_Cover: AI BOM/Tech-Pack Generator + Supply Chain Network
slide_2_Idea_Problem_Solution_Value: Sample development compressed from 6 months to 51 days (10x faster) — proven in paid ENNO functional-apparel PoC with contract conversion achieved
Automated BOM accuracy reduces defect rates by 5-15% and eliminates $800 rework cost per style, removing hidden supply-chain waste at each iteration
SME brands access enterprise-tier supplier networks at VF Corp caliber at startup-friendly pricing — eliminating the network moat of large corporations
Ski and snowboard brands manually create Bill Of Materials(BOM) and Technical Package(specifications) over 6+ months, causing severe time-to-market bottlenecks during critical growth phases
Functional sourcing requires waterproof, breathable, thermal fabrics but low-MOQ factories are scarce — manual supplier matching alone wastes 2-3 months
Existing alternatives are fragmented: traditional agencies charge high fees with analog workflows, while platforms like FAAI only handle fast fashion
AI engine converts design images to factory-ready BOM and Tech-Pack specs — eliminating the manual 4-week authoring cycle for technical apparel entirely
Korea-Vietnam dual R&D infrastructure with Seoul IT validation and Vietnam production enables rapid prototype iteration at global manufacturing scale
Constraint-aware automated PO generation dispatches to pre-validated suppliers — compressing procurement from 4+ weeks to 1-2 days at production scale
slide_3_Market_1_3_TAM_SAM_SOM_+_Why_Now: Korea offers best-in-class IT talent plus Seoul quick-turn apparel clusters — ecosystem density supports rapid MVP validation for technical apparel development
McKinsey confirms brands diversifying from China to Vietnam and India — EU ESPR textiles timeline 2027 adds traceability demand for documented supply chains
Manual BOM and Tech-Pack authoring is a universal bottleneck across all functional-apparel markets — Korea-Vietnam dual R&D infrastructure is asset-light
Global outdoor & functional apparel development and production management
$1T global apparel market × 5~15% take care = ~$100B addressable service market for AI agentic development and production management
Production management services are embedded in every order — making this a recurring, structurally defensible market regardless of brand size
SME-sized brands represent ~20% of TAM ($1T) — too small for enterprise supply chain tools, too complex for DIY management
This segment lacks dedicated production management partners — creating a structural gap that a XaaS model is built to fill

At $1B in intermediated production volume and a 5% conservative take rate, this implies a $50M–$200M+ revenue opportunity, with further upside driven by demand aggregation, supply-side integration, and workflow lock-in
slide_4_Market_2_3_Competition: Enterprise PLM expanding to emerging brands with AI modules — strongest incumbent already covering SMB and outdoor segments - legacy ERP style solution
Intelligent garment production platform with 155K+ brands — directory matching without tech pack generation or production QA - B2B marketplace
Tech-enabled manufacturing platform with 100+ US brands and design-to-delivery — proves category with larger capital base - humans in the loop with digital tools
AI fashion generator with tech pack automation and 3D design — commoditizes AI BOM generation as a standalone feature - design prototype layer
Domain-specific BOM AI for technical apparel converts design images to production-ready specs — competitors offer generic fashion tools without functional-wear expertise
Integrated SaaS plus XaaS production in Vietnam with constraint-aware sourcing — competitors lack production infrastructure forcing brands to source separately
Sourcing-first GTM proves complexity and ROI before upsell to full suite — agencies charge upfront fees while platforms offer matching without execution quality
slide_5_Market_3_3_GTM_Pricing_BM_Scale: SaaS $100/seat/month plus production commission 20% of FOB per style
Freemium SaaS entry enables low-friction onboarding before XaaS upsell
Blended ACV $70K per brand (SaaS $1.2K/yr plus XaaS production GMV)
Recurring SaaS plus high-margin production commission creates compounding revenue as customer cohorts scale
Domain expertise and supplier network moat with 60%+ gross margin create defensible pricing in technical niche
Stage 1: Dominate ski and snowboard segment via sourcing-first motion — proves complexity and ROI then upsells to full design plus BOM plus production suite
Stage 2: Expand to broader outdoor and functional-wear verticals like gym, running, and winter sports leveraging Phase 1 playbook and factory network
Stage 3: Global brand recruitment via Korea-Vietnam dual R&D center with API integrations to Adobe, CLO3D, and major ERP platforms for workflow lock-in
End-to-end design-to-BOM-to-PO-to-production pipeline automated — unit economics improve as customer production volumes scale across seasons
Shared factory data across cohorts trains ML models for BOM accuracy — new material onboarding accelerates from weeks to days with each iteration
Sequential expansion from ski and snowboard to broader outdoor to global premium brands via density-driven factory partnerships and proven playbook
slide_6_Team_1_4_Composition_&_Roles: CEO Sunghan brings 10yr+ VF Corp global sourcing experience including Hanoi branch operations — rare supply-chain domain depth with direct factory network access and MOQ expertise
CTO Aleksandr combines AI/ML systems architecture with materials science dual expertise — currently located in Israel and did not relocate to Korea and is a relative black box
Co-founders met in Singapore graduate school and spent years collaborating on supply-chain automation — deep foundational trust built through extended academic partnership
CEO leads GTM and fundraising leveraging direct factory relationships globally while CTO architects the hybrid AI engine and dual Korea-Vietnam R&D infrastructure end-to-end

Led Hanoi Branch operations; built factory networks with deep MOQ expertise
Identified problem from direct exposure to brand-factory friction at scale
Sportmaster Singapore R&D; IT and Materials Science dual degree holder
Relocated Israel to Korea to solve BOM-generation technical bottleneck
Architected hybrid AI engine for functional-wear BOM from design images
slide_7_Team_2_4_Program_Trajectory: External co-founder preformed 2-person team — CEO from VF Corp with 10yr sourcing and CTO is AI architect with materials science dual expertise from Singapore
Met in Singapore grad school as the only two in their cohort passionate about apparel industry — years of collaboration on supply-chain automation problems
CEO identified pain from VF Corp field experience and CTO relocated Israel to Korea to solve core BOM bottleneck — extreme commitment signal from both founders
Bootcamp1 rejected — Jiho feedback: problem and solution too broad, need specific customer persona and workflow. Began narrowing ICP to ski and snowboard niche
Bootcamp3-1 rejected but Bootcamp3-2 scored 9 and approved by Jiho for trackout on April 1 — problem and solution definition simplified while scalability fully preserved
Trackout started April 1 targeting ENNO paid PoC for functional running-wear and ISPO Munich for international lead generation as key validation milestones
ENNO PoC delivered 51-day proto-sample versus 6-month industry standard — ISPO Munich yielded 25 qualified international leads and CTO relocated full-time
Summarizes the journey from program start through team formation and PMF validation, based on weekly founder retrospectives and program team observations
slide_8_Team_3_4_Evaluation: Singapore grad-school bond. 75/25 equity clear. CTO not fully vetted during residency as he did not relocate to participate.
ENNO paid PoC: 51 days vs 6 months. ISPO Munich: 25 leads. Korea-Vietnam dual R&D live. Contract converted. However the team has been working on this for over a year before Antler.
Bootcamp1 red to Bootcamp3-1 rejected to Bootcamp3-2 score 9 approved. Iterative ICP narrowing from broad apparel to ski niche
CEO 10yr VF Corp supply-chain ops. Hanoi branch experience. Deep factory networks, MOQ, cost, brand pain known
Hybrid AI for BOM from design images. However the software itself does not seem to be a strong moat.
Domain-specific BOM AI for functional apparel is novel. SaaS plus XaaS integrated model beats directory-only
Based on weekly founder retrospectives and feedback
slide_9_Team_4_4_Strengths_&_Improvement: Strengths
● Problem validated by existing players
Apparelmark, Sewport, Fashinza all monetize the same pain — technical apparel development is slow and error-prone
● ENNO PoC proves 10x speed improvement
51-day proto-sample versus 6-month standard with contract conversion — strongest early execution signal in cohort
● Supply chain tailwind is externally real
McKinsey confirms China diversification to Vietnam; EU ESPR textiles timeline 2027 adds traceability demand
● Founder-problem fit from VF Corp depth
CEO 10yr sourcing at VF Corp with Hanoi branch ops — domain language and factory network access are genuine
Areas for Improvement
● AI tech pack category already crowded
Centric, Lectra, Style3D all offer AI-assisted tech packs — BOM generation alone is not a defensible moat
● Service intensity may not decrease
Tech design review, sample revision, factory QA all require human involvement — agency economics risk persists
● CTO commitment risk
CTO long-term commitment and drive to build the business still unproven
Team (4/4)
Summary
● Equity is split 75% (CEO) and 25% (CTO), balancing founder-driven control with strong incentives for technical ownership and execution
Founder Alignment & Equity Structure
slide_10_Investor_Assessment: Large, structurally recurring market if they move from tooling into a transaction-linked supply chain layer.
$1T manufacturing base supports ~$100B service layer, only if they aggregate demand and control supply.
Strong SMB wedge with clear gaps between legacy PLM and marketplaces.
Expansion into larger markets will face entrenched incumbents and well-capitalized platforms.
Near-term defensibility comes from workflow integration and switching costs in an underserved niche.
Expansion into larger markets will face entrenched incumbents and well-capitalized platforms.
Credible early traction with paid PoC and meaningful cycle time improvement.
Still at micro scale with low margins and limited throughput, some questions on execution speed pre-Antler.
Highly scalable in theory given fragmented global supply chains and standardized workflows.
Execution risk remains in building supply-side integration and achieving real GMV aggregation.
Strong founder-problem fit with deep domain expertise and relevant supply chain experience.
Some uncertainty around CTO commitment and ability to execute at required technical depth.

Large, structurally recurring supply chain opportunity with a credible wedge in an underserved SMB segment. Early traction validates the problem, but success depends on evolving from tooling into a GMV-linked execution layer. Strong domain-driven founder, with key risks in supply aggregation, execution scale, and technical leadership. Inevitabile solution / More to be desired from Team
